findings.evidence: `rg` shows Mathlib already has `theorem preimage_iterate {s : Set α} (h : IsFixedPt (Set.preimage f) s) (n : ℕ) : IsFixedPt (Set.preimage f^[n]) s` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Dynamics/FixedPoints/Basic.lean:69`; this PR specializes it to `(shift α)^[n] ⁻¹' s = s` at lines 76-78.
findings.fix: Use `Function.IsFixedPt.preimage_iterate` directly at call sites, or add any missing general lemma around `IsFixedPt (Set.preimage f)` first and make this only a thin local alias if the roadmap truly needs the name.
findings.issue: `IsShiftInvariant.iterate` restates the general preimage-iterate fixed-point theorem for the special case `f = shift α`.

findings.evidence: `rg` shows `theorem le_invariants_iterate (f : α → α) (n : ℕ) : invariants f ≤ invariants (f^[n])` at `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/MeasurableSpace/Invariants.lean:55`; the PR specializes the fixed-set conclusion to `shift α` at lines 82-85.
findings.fix: Derive this from `MeasurableSpace.le_invariants_iterate` or expose a general helper for arbitrary `f : α → α`, then keep only the necessary shift adapter.
findings.issue: `MeasurableSet.shift_iterate_preimage_eq` is another shift-specific iterate invariant statement, while Mathlib already proves the natural invariant-sigma iterate relation for any self-map.

summary: The iterate API is added at a shift-specific level even though Mathlib already has the natural general fixed-point/invariants lemmas. Keep the roadmap-facing adapters only after factoring through the general API, or drop the redundant special cases.
